headline: Micron drops 6% premarket amid tech rout
detail: Micron's premarket drop signals broad chip sector selling pressure today. This directly threatens AVGO, SMH, and CRWD — your chip and tech-heavy names. On margin, even a moderate sector-wide move down accelerates drawdowns fast.

headline: U.S. stocks see first outflow since March, risk-off summer ahead
detail: Institutional money rotating out is a headwind for high-beta tech and chip names — exactly your book. Risk-off summers historically punish concentrated, leveraged retail positions more than diversified ones. This supports trimming losers now rather than waiting for a bounce.

headline: Oil may fall below $50 as OPEC loses control
detail: Cheap oil is a mild macro positive for tech (lower input/energy costs) but the bigger story is what it signals — slowing global demand and deflationary pressure. In a risk-off environment, that macro backdrop doesn't help sentiment for growth names like PLTR or CRWD.
